items.title: Microsoft details next-gen hybrid PC Xbox console at GDC: Specs, backward compatibility, potentially wild price tag, more
items.summary:
Many of the rumors surrounding the next-generation Xbox are, at least seemingly, coming true. Microsoft and the newly appointed head of Xbox have already confirmed Project Helix is well into development and that it will indeed be able to play both Xbox and PC games. What that means exactly is still unclear, but we do have new details directly from Xbox on everything from a hint at the release window, the machine’s dual-functionality, and even some actual specs here today. 

more…

items.summary: General Catalyst is said to be raising $10 billion and Spark Capital $3 billion. This after several other firms have also raised mega-funds.

items.title: Poppi founder on TikTok, Super Bowl ads, and her return to Shark Tank
items.summary: For years, venture capitalists have been skeptical of beverage startups, citing thin margins and brutal distribution as reasons most brands never break out. But a new wave of “functional soda” companies has been challenging that assumption, including Poppi, the prebiotic soda brand that grew from a kitchen experiment into a $1.95 billion acquisition by PepsiCo.  On this episode of […]

items.summary:

The DELTA Pro Ultra X on its own carries a $10,597 MSRP direct from the brand, but starts off lower at $8,299 here, with this exclusive deal mimicking the one that held on for the first two months of 2026 – first at $6,999, then later at $6,499. If you missed out on that previous deal, you’re getting the next-best price here with the $1,300 savings, not to mention the free compact TRAIL 200 DC station that carries a $200 MSRP but can more often be scored down between $139 and $109 on average. Head below to learn all about these two backup power solutions while the deal continues.

items.title: It doesn’t get much less than $130 for a 27-inch 170Hz Acer WQHD gaming monitor, score this model at 48% off
items.summary:
Over at Newegg, you’ll find Acer’s 27-inch Nitro WQHD 170Hz gaming monitor currently going for $129.99 shipped. You’re looking at clearance pricing here as Acer has already rolled out newer models, including a 27-inch 240Hz version that is currently selling for $250. Today’s deal chops $120 off its $250 list price, landing at roughly a 48% discount. Not only is this the lowest price we can currently find for this model, but it’s also a solid price for a WQHD gaming display – it doesn’t get much less than this for a QHD monitor. This is a great option to consider if you’re putting together a budget-friendly battlestation, and you can learn more about it below.

more…

items.title: Revive batteries, inflate tires, charge devices, and more with this GOOLOO A3 jump starter for $70
items.summary:
GOOLOO A3 Jump Starter with Air Compressor

Buy for $70 ($110 list price)

What we love

Not only are you gaining a handy means to jump start dead car batteries with the GOOLOO A3 model, which is particularly useful during extreme winter and summer weather, but you’re also getting a built-in air compressor here for your tires, too. It’s able to hit a max 150 PSI through its five different modes tailored for specific tire types (SUV, car, bike, ball, and DIY), and even comes with added functionality to act as a power bank, LED flashlight, and an S.O.S. emergency signal.

While this model carries a $170 MSRP from Govee’s website, since the day following Christmas Amazon had it priced higher at $200, which dropped back down to its regular going rate in late February, and is now dropping further here. The 41% markdown cuts $70 off the tag to land it back at the all-time lowest price we have tracked for the first time since it last appeared for Christmas sales. You can also browse the brand’s other ongoing smart lighting deals in the official Amazon storefront here, or via the main website’s sale page here.

Regularly priced at $50, today’s deal knocks $15 off for a solid 30% discount. It’s available in black, blue, pink, and white, and all colorways are down to $35 right now. While the black model briefly dipped to $33 during Black Friday, today’s offer marks the lowest price we’ve tracked for the other finishes. You also get a 5-foot USB-C cable in the box with these chargers, so you’re looking at a solid overall value at its discounted price. Head below for more details.

The price here is only beaten out by falls to $208 that last happened in September, as well as the one-time drop to the $197 low that we spotted during early October’s Prime Day event. Jumping on it now gives you a 30% markdown that cuts $90 off the going rate, landing it at the best price we have tracked in nearly five months – including beating out Black Friday pricing.
